extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won bunch sprint after JUMBO-VISMA and QUICK-STEP created echelons that split race, dropping GC contenders MCNULTY, ALMEIDA, and SCHACHMANN 2:05 back
tactical_patterns: JUMBO-VISMA and QUICK-STEP cooperation in echelon splits reveals tactical alignment despite competing sprint interests, prioritizing GC damage.
BISSEGER solo attack inside 8km drew JUMBO's LAPORTE into neutralizing work, disrupting QUICK-STEP's train without preventing JAKOBSEN victory.
Second group featuring JORGENSON and CORTINA closed to 20 seconds before final crosswinds split fragmented, showing echelon timing crucial.
Front group re-fractured when turning into more wind in final 20km despite previous regrouping, indicating sustained pace control.

rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: Dropped in initial echelon split alongside fellow GC contenders, losing 2:05 on stage 2 reveals poor positioning instincts
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Lost 2:05 in echelons with SCHACHMANN, suggesting Trek-Segafredo team structure inadequate for wind protection
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: negative: Defending champion dropped 2:05 in crosswinds, catastrophic GC loss on stage 2 indicates poor team support or form
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Made front echelon split and stayed attentive through multiple accelerations, unexpected crosswind savvy for pure climber
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Initially distanced but regrouped with front before final split, survived multiple echelon selections despite climbing specialist profile
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: neutral: Yellow jersey holder pulled to chase BISSEGER attack inside 8km, sacrificing sprint position for team defense
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Named alongside ROGLIC as GC contender making all front selections, UAE positioning strength notable
